factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, supported by strong orders and backlog growth.

evidence.text: For the quarter ended September 30, 2018, the Company posted strong orders, backlog, sales, operating income, operating income margins, net income, diluted earnings per share and operating cash flow. The Company achieved these results from organic sales growth in both EIG and EMG, contributions from the acquisitions of Motec in June 2018, SoundCom in April 2018, FMH in January 2018 and Arizona Instrument LLC in December 2017, as well as the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, indicating strong demand.

evidence.text: As a result, the Company's backlog of unfilled orders at September 30, 2018 was $1,589.3 million, an increase of $193.2 million or 13.8%, compared with $1,396.1 million at December 31, 2017.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong sales performance across segments.

evidence.text: For 2018, positive market trends, the Company's strong backlog, the full year impact of the 2018 and 2017 acquisitions and continued focus on and implementation of Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on the remainder of the Company's 2018 results.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net sales for the third quarter of 2018 were $1,193.0 million, an increase of $108.2 million or 10.0%, compared with net sales of $1,084.8 million for the third quarter of 2017. The increase in net sales for the third quarter of 2018 was due to 7% organic sales growth and a 3% increase from acquisitions.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by increased sales and cost efficiency.

evidence.text: Segment operating income, as a percentage of net sales, increased to 23.7% for the first nine months of 2018, compared with 23.1% for the first nine months of 2017. The increase in segment operating income and segment operating margins for the first nine months of 2018 resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to improved margins.

evidence.text: The increase in segment operating income and segment operating margins for the first nine months of 2018 resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by Operational Excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: The increase in segment operating income and segment operating margins for the first nine months of 2018 resulted primarily from the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.

factors.summary: Management has maintained positive forward guidance, expecting continued growth.

evidence.text: For 2018, positive market trends, the Company's strong backlog, the full year impact of the 2018 and 2017 acquisitions and continued focus on and implementation of Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on the remainder of the Company's 2018 results.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as indicated by increased dividends and expansion of credit facilities.

evidence.text: In the fourth quarter of 2018, $65 million of 7.18% senior notes will mature and become payable. The debt-to-capital ratio was 29.6% at September 30, 2018, compared with 35.1% at December 31, 2017. The net debt-to-capital ratio was 23.5% at September 30, 2018, compared with 27.5% at December 31, 2017. Effective February 1, 2018, the Company's Board of Directors approved a 56% increase in the quarterly cash dividend on the Company's common stock to $0.14 per common share from $0.09 per common share.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign currency translation effects and potential supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Foreign currency translation was essentially flat period over period. The Company is in compliance with all covenants, including financial covenants, for all of its debt agreements. The Company believes it has sufficient cash-generating capabilities from domestic and unrestricted foreign sources, available credit facilities and access to long-term capital funds to enable it to meet its operating needs and contractual obligations in the foreseeable future.
